assertions.expectation: Defines 'available' as versions officially available in the channel that customers can manually upgrade to
assertions.reason: Response clearly defines 'available' as 'Versions you CAN manually upgrade to right now' and explains it as 'your menu of options'

assertions.expectation: Defines 'default' as the version used for new cluster creation — NOT necessarily the auto-upgrade target
assertions.reason: Response explicitly defines 'default' as 'Version used for NEW cluster creation' and emphasizes the distinction from auto-upgrade target

assertions.expectation: Defines 'auto-upgrade target' as the version GKE will actually upgrade existing clusters to automatically
assertions.reason: Response clearly defines 'auto-upgrade target' as 'Version GKE will automatically upgrade YOUR existing cluster to'

assertions.expectation: Explains that default and auto-upgrade target are often the same but CAN differ, especially during new minor version rollouts
assertions.reason: Response explains the difference between default (1.29.8) and auto-upgrade target (1.29.7) and mentions this occurs during rollouts, though it doesn't explicitly state they're 'often the same'

assertions.expectation: Notes that the auto-upgrade target depends on cluster constraints (maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly lists 'Maintenance windows' and 'Maintenance exclusions' as factors that determine the auto-upgrade target

assertions.expectation: Explains that a cluster with a 'no minor' exclusion will have a patch-only auto-upgrade target for its current minor
assertions.reason: Response mentions maintenance exclusions as a factor but does not specifically explain the 'no minor' exclusion scenario or patch-only behavior

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking the cluster's auto-upgrade status to see the actual target version
assertions.reason: Response provides specific gcloud commands to check the cluster's auto-upgrade target and status

assertions.expectation: Points to the GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
assertions.reason: Response does not mention or reference the GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
